facts.text: Broadway, museums, galleries, and festivals line New York City’s streets, inviting visitors to explore its rich cultural history.
Harlem hosts ten arts institutions and cultural centers, making it a vibrant hub of Uptown Manhattan’s historic neighborhood.
Staten Island, New York City’s southernmost borough, offers historic sites, beach views, and global dining—all reachable by a short ferry ride.
The Bronx, one of New York City’s boroughs, is packed with history and culture that shaped the city’s identity.
NYC Restaurant Week began in 1992, forever changing the city’s dining scene by spotlighting local chefs each week.
The song “Theme from New York, New York,” composed by John Kander and Fred Ebb, became the anthem of the 1977 film by Martin Scorsese.
